subject: Social Science
chapter_name: India – Geographical Position and Physical Features
learning_outcomes: Explain the geographical location, area, land and water borders of India
Describe the natural features of India
Name the natural divisions of India

key_terms.definition: The Great Himalayas — always covered with snow
Flat narrow plains in the foothills of the Himalayas
The oldest and largest geographical division of India — rich in minerals
The extreme southern tip of India

mcqs.question: The largest geographical division of India is
mcqs.explanation: The Peninsular Plateau is the largest geographical division of India

mcqs.question: Highest mountain peak of India
mcqs.explanation: K2 (Godwin Austin) at 8611m is the highest peak in India

mcqs.question: The Eastern Coastal Plain is
mcqs.explanation: The Eastern Coastal Plain is wide and flat

mcqs.question: Mount Everest lies between
mcqs.explanation: Mount Everest lies on the border between Nepal and Tibet

one_mark_questions.question: Where do the Eastern and Western Ghats meet?
Why is the North Indian Plain called the 'deposition plain'?
one_mark_questions.answer: It is formed by the deposition of alluvial soils brought by the rivers of Himalayas

one_mark_questions.question: Which is the extreme southern tip of India?
On which longitude is the Indian Standard Time based?
By what name are the Western Ghats called in Maharashtra and Karnataka?
Which is the highest mountain peak in South India?

three_mark_questions.question: Which are the major natural divisions of India?
How are the Himalayas useful for Indians?
answer: Block the cold winds from North Asia
Are suitable for hydroelectric power generation
Are famous for tourism and religious sites
Are a haven for flora and fauna
